channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Who will win The Open? Sky Sports pundit predictions and have your say!
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Each of the last 12 editions of The Open have been won by a first-time champion, but will that run continue this week and who could challenge the world’s best for major victory?

item.title: What do Antonelli, Russell & Hamilton need to do in F1 title battle?
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: With a potentially thrilling title battle shaping up as the 2026 Formula 1 season nears its halfway point, Sky Sports F1 assesses the prospects of the main contenders.

item.title: Golf 'coming home' too?! England's stars face World Cup dilemma at The Open
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: In a week where England look to end their 60-year wait for World Cup glory, the nation’s golfers also aim break their own lengthy winless run at The Open.

item.title: Defend Students’ Interests, Federal Govt Tells NANS Leadership
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Federal government has challenged the newly inaugurated leadership of the National Association of Nigerian Students (NANS) to champion safer campuses, protect students’ interests and promote constructive engagement with government and educational institutions. The charge was given at the i

item.title: Imo assembly moves against NJC’s continuous extension of state acting chief judge
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Imo State House of Assembly has codemned the decision of National Judicial Commission, NJC, over what it described as continuous extension of the appointment  of the acting Chief Judge of the State. It alleged that the action violated the provisions  of section 271(5) of the 1999 Constitut

item.title: Minister Engages Chinese Industries To Drive Technology Transfer
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Minister of Defence, General Christopher Musa (rtd) has led a delegation to China for a high-level engagements on defence, security cooperation, and strategic partnership between the Federal Republic of Nigeria and the People’s Republic of China. The Minister in a statement by his Special Advise

item.title: Tinubu Support Group Distributes 200 Bags Of Fertiliser To Farmers In Kaduna
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: No fewer than 200 farmers in Kaduna State are to benefit from the distribution of fertiliser under an agricultural support initiative of the Tinubu Support Group (TSG), aimed at boosting food production during the current farming season. The intervention, flagged off in Kaduna on Monday, is part of

item.title: Ballon d’Or 2026 ranking: Mbappe’s hopes fade as Yamal shines in World Cup semi-final
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The hopes of France captain, Kylian Mbappe, winning the 2026 Ballon d’Or took a big dent on Tuesday night, after they lost 2-0 to Spain in the semi-final of the World Cup. On the other hand, Lamine Yamal boosted his chances of finishing on the podium, as he helped his team qualify fo

item.title: Political Party De-registration And Judicial Contradictions
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: As African Democratic Congress (ADC) and Nigerian Democratic Congress (NDC) hang on the courts for breathe of life, we reflash this editorial to refresh our minds about the implication for democracy itself The recent judicial pronouncement ordering the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC

item.title: FG to review Chinese firm’s proposal to build 10,000 homes in 30 months
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Federal Government has commenced a review of a proposal by China Hyway Group Limited to develop 10,000 housing units across Nigeria within 30 months. The post FG to review Chinese firm’s proposal to build 10,000 homes in 30 months appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.title: Spain shuts down France and advances to the World Cup final with a 2-0 victory
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Spain’s defensive prowess and swagger were just too much for an attacking trio led by France great Kylian Mbappé, and just enough to get the 2010 champions into another World Cup final. Spain will play on Sunday against the winner between reigning champion Argentina and England on ⁠Wednesday. France

item.title: Senator Natasha Congratulates Rescued Oyo Victims, Calls For Trauma Support
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Senator Natasha Akpoti-Uduaghan, representing Kogi Central Senatorial District, has congratulated the rescued schoolchildren, teachers and their families following their release after more than 50 days in captivity in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State. The lawmaker, while contributing to a m

item.title: World Cup: French dream comes to an end against dominating Spain
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The French team were defeated by Spain (2–0) in the World Cup semi-final. France’s dream of reaching a third consecutive final – a feat achieved by only two other nations before them – has come to an end.

item.title: Offa Robbery: Saraki’s Lawyers Tell Judge To Recuse Himself
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Kwara State High Court yesterday adjourned till 21 July 2026 for the hearing of the motion on notice filed by lawyers to former Senate President, Dr Abubakar Bukola Saraki, requesting the judge presiding over the Offa robbery case filed by the state government against Saraki and his successor, A

item.title: Nigerian Newspapers: 10 things you need to know Wednesday morning
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Good morning! Here is today’s summary from Nigerian Newspapers: 1. Adeniyi Adeyemi, Director-General of the controversial Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council, PFIPC, has been arrested. He was arrested in Osun State hours after a Federal High Court sitting in Abuja ordered his immedia

item.title: South Africa: Health Department Ramps Up Ebola Readiness - South African News Briefs - July 15, 2026

item.description: [allAfrica]

item.title: Fresh Benue attack leaves one dead
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Suspected armed herders attacked Ondo community in Benue, killing one person. Learn more about the latest Benue attack and ongoing security efforts.
Read More: https://punchng.com/fresh-benue-attack-leaves-one-dead/

item.title: Reps dump own state police bill, adopt Tinubu’s version
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The House of Representatives has withdrawn its State Police Bill, opting to adopt President Tinubu’s more robust version for constitutional amendment. Lear
Read More: https://punchng.com/reps-dump-own-state-police-bill-adopt-tinubus-version/

item.title: Benue To Establish Grain Reserve System, Trains Farmers In Ginger Production
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Benue State Ministry of Agriculture and Food Security has disclosed plans to establish a State Grain Reserve System with storage facilities in the 23 Local Government Areas to enhance food security, reduce post-harvest losses, and stabilise the prices of agricultural commodities. The Commissioner fo

item.title: Technical education, Nigeria’s fastest job creation route – NABTEB

item.description:
NABTEB declares technical education as Nigeria’s strongest pathway to employment and economic growth, equipping youth with practical skills for job creatio

item.title: FG grants Shell $11.5/barrel tax credit to unlock $20bn investment
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
FG grants Shell a special $11.5/barrel tax credit for the Bonga Southwest Aparo project, aiming to unlock $20bn investment and boost oil production.
Read More: https://punchng.com/fg-grants-shell-11-5-barrel-tax-credit-to-unlock-20bn-investment/

item.title: China economic growth falls sharply, missing target
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Weak demand domestically and the impact of the Iran war on oil prices overshadowed the country's strong exports.

item.title: De-registration: Appeal Court reserves judgment in ADC, 4 others’ suit
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Justice Lifu had ordered INEC to deregister the five political parties on the grounds that they failed to meet the constitutional requirements necessary to retain their registration.

item.title: Suleiman, Teriba, Uwaleke, 12 Others To Advise Federal Govt On Economic Reforms
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The federal government has inaugurated a Ministerial Advisory Committee to provide independent, evidence-based advice to the Ministry of Finance as it seeks to translate ongoing economic reforms into measurable improvements in the lives of Nigerians. The minister of Finance and coordinating minister

item.title: Dangote Refinery Begins Pricing Local Fuel Sales In Dollars
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigeria’s downstream petroleum sector is bracing for renewed foreign exchange (FX) pressure after Dangote Petroleum Refinery scrapped its naira-denominated pricing model for petrol, diesel and aviation fuel, replacing it with a dollar-based framework that ties domestic fuel prices directly to

item.title: State Policing, Power Devolution Key To Stronger Security Architecture, Say Speakers
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Conference of Speakers of State Legislatures of Nigeria has said the ongoing constitutional amendment process on State Policing and Devolution of Powers has the potential to strengthen Nigeria’s security architecture. Speaking at the opening ceremony of the 2026 National Assembly Open Week

item.title: MTN invests in youth entrepreneurship ecosystem in Kano
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: MTN Nigeria has expanded its investment in nationwide youth engagement initiative, The Gathering 100, to Kano, bringing together young innovators, read more MTN invests in youth entrepreneurship ecosystem in Kano

item.title: Airtel Africa ramps up 1.8m share buyback as stock rallies
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Airtel Africa Plc has bought back close to two million of its own shares over the course of a single read more Airtel Africa ramps up 1.8m share buyback as stock rallies

item.title: Oriire: Scholars, professionals push for special judicial panels to try suspects
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: ELDER statesmen, scholars and other professionals under The Yoruba Leadership and Peace Initiative (TYLPI) on Monday urged President Bola Tinubu to facilitate the establishment of special judicial panels across the six geopolitical zones to expedite prosecution of terrorists. The post Oriire: Schola

item.title: Oriire abduction: Akume says Tinubu displayed sagacity, berates Makinde
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Akume avers that the successful rescue operation underscores the Tinubu Administration's unwavering commitment to safeguarding the lives and property of Nigerians through well-coordinated security operations rather than public grandstanding.

item.title: Mates, mate and freedom - how Argentina got the best out of Messi
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Guillem Balague examines how the Argentina squad is built around Lionel Messi as they prepare to play against England in the World Cup semi-finals.

item.title: I still feel pain of '98 but this can be different for England - Shearer
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England's defeat by Argentina at the 1998 World Cup still burns deep for Alan Shearer, but he explains why this team can take their chance to change their lives forever.

item.title: England's biggest match since 1966 as Three Lions close in on immortality
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England face Argentina in the World Cup semi-finals on Wednesday - and BBC Sport's Phil McNulty explains why it is the Three Lions' biggest match since 1966.

item.title: T.Rex skeleton sells for $50.1 million at New York auction
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: A T.Rex skeleton nicknamed ‘Gus’ sold for a record $50 million at an auction in New York.

item.title: Africa: Global Childhood Immunization Coverage Inches Forward Despite Conflict and Hesitancy - Unicef, WHO
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [WHO] In 2025, 90% of infants globally - or nearly 116 million - received at least one dose of a diphtheria, tetanus and pertussis (DTP) vaccine, and 85% - or 110 million - completed the full three-dose series, according to the annual WHO-UNICEF Estimates of National Immunization Coverage (WUENIC) r

item.title: WORLD IN BRIEF: Singapore court orders Bloomberg to pay $356,000, Brussels construction fire kills six, Kenya extends fuel tax relief and other stories
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Trump drops Hormuz cargo fee as US intensifies blockade of Iranian ports Donald Trump, US President has abandoned plans to read more WORLD IN BRIEF: Singapore court orders Bloomberg to pay $356,000, Brussels construction fire kills six, Kenya extends fuel tax relief and other stories

item.title: 2026 World Cup final: Spain coach, De La Fuente names best team in the world
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Spain head coach, Luis de la Fuente, has claimed that his side are “the best team in the world”. De La Fuente was speaking to the media after they beat France 2-0 in the semi-final of the 2026 World Cup. Mikel Oyarzabal and Pedro Porro scored in either half as La Roja qualified for the [

item.title: Kenya: Nairobi Metropolitan Police to Launch in Coming Days, Ruto Says
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- President William Ruto has announced that the rollout of the Nairobi Metropolitan Policing Framework will commence in the coming days as part of efforts to strengthen security and community policing across the capital and its surrounding areas.

item.title: Sudan: EU Bans Sudan Gold to Target War Economy
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Dabanga] Brussels -- The Council of the European Union on Monday adopted a decision banning the purchase, import, or transport of gold originating from Sudan. It also prohibited the sale, supply, transfer or export of mercury and cyanide to Sudan. These chemicals are widely used in gold mining and

item.title: Reps withdraw passed State Police Bill as Tinubu sends fresh Constitution Amendment proposal
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has transmitted to the House of Representatives a fresh Constitution Alteration Bill seeking to establish a legal read more Reps withdraw passed State Police Bill as Tinubu sends fresh Constitution Amendment proposal

item.title: 2026 World Cup: Ian Wright reveals who to blame for France’s 2-0 defeat to Spain
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Arsenal striker, Ian Wright, has blamed France players for their 2-0 defeat to Spain in the semi-final of the 2026 World Cup. Wright, speaking on ITV, claimed that structure trumped individuality in the game. Mikel Oyarzabal and Pedro Porro scored for Spain as they eased into the final, where

item.title: Congo-Kinshasa: 'This Is a Fire' - DR Congo Ebola Outbreak Is Fastest-Growing Ever, Warns WHO
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [UN News] Infections of the Bundibugyo species of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) have reached record highs and a majority of new cases are coming from "unknown chains of transmission", the UN World Health Organization (WHO) warned on Tuesday.

item.title: 2026 World Cup: We’re in shock – Gary Neville reacts as Spain beat France 2-0
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Manchester United defender, Gary Neville, has expressed shock after Spain beat France 2-0 in the semi-final of the 2026 World Cup. Les Bleus came into the game as favourites, but will now settle for the third-place play-off. Mikel Oyazarbal’s penalty and Pedro Porro’s composed fin

item.title: Author unveils ‘The Proverbial Seven Pillars of Wisdom’
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The public presentation of The Proverbial Seven Pillars of Wisdom, authored by Reverend Dr Felix Tenimimonimi El Schaeddhaei, JP, drew religious leaders, academics, professionals, politicians, and other dignitaries to celebrate a work centred on wisdom and quality leadership. The post Author unveils

item.title: Almajiri budget for roads across the country?
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: REPORTS by national newspapers that the National Commission for Almajiri and Out-of-School Children’s Education (NCAOOSCE) received budgetary allocations totaling about ₦22.4 billion over the 2025 and 2026 fiscal years The post Almajiri budget for roads across the country? appeared first on Tribune

item.title: FG Inaugurates Advisory Committee To Drive Economic Reforms
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Federal Government has inaugurated a Ministerial Advisory Committee (MAC) to provide strategic guidance on Nigeria's fiscal and macroeconomic reforms, with a focus on strengthening economic growth, public financial management and evidence-based policymaking. The post FG Inaugurates Advisory Comm

item.title: Party primaries: We legislated against ourselves, says Akpabio
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Senate President, Godswill Akpabio, has said the National Assembly may review the Electoral Act 2026, admitting that lawmakers "legislated against themselves" by introducing provisions that left many incumbent legislators unable to secure their parties' nominations for the 2027 elections. The post P

item.title: Adejuwon charges new NANS leadership to prioritise student welfare, autonomy
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The new National Executive Council of the National Association of Nigerian Students (NANS) led by Comrade Babatunde Akinteye was on Tuesday inaugurated in Abuja with stakeholders describing the ceremony as the beginning of a new era for the student body. The post Adejuwon charges new NANS leadership

item.title: Senate urges FG to intensify military operations against terrorists, bandits
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Senate has urged the Federal Government to sustain and intensify ongoing military and intelligence operations against terrorists, kidnappers, bandits and other criminal elements across the country. The post Senate urges FG to intensify military operations against terrorists, bandits appeared fir

item.title: Oil hits one-month high of $86.79 as US-Iran conflict escalates
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Global crude oil prices climbed sharply on Tuesday, hitting their highest level in a month after fresh military hostilities between the United States and Iran raised fears of disruptions to global oil supplies through the strategic Strait of Hormuz. The post Oil hits one-month high of $86.79 as US-I

item.title: NYSC threatens to blacklist hostile corps employers over welfare, accommodation
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The management of National Youth Service Corps (NYSC), has cautioned all corps employers to upgrade and prioritise the welfare of corps members posted to serve in their establishments, warning that any employer who did not provide accommodation and transportation for corps members would be blacklist

item.title: Police arrest Adeyemi, PFIPC DG, in Osun
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Nigeria Police yesterday arrested Prince Adeniyi Adeyemi, the self-styled Director-General of the alleged “non-existent” Presidential Foreign Investment Promotion Council (PFIPC). The post Police arrest Adeyemi, PFIPC DG, in Osun appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Presidency reaffirms commitment to productive relationship with NASS
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The presidency has reaffirmed its commitment to a productive relationship with the National Assembly in pursuit of security, economic renewal, social justice and shared prosperity under the Renewed Hope Agenda. The post Presidency reaffirms commitment to productive relationship with NASS appeared fi

item.title: Windows into life, love, and longing
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Poetic Meditations, Inspired, by Professor Ayo Elebute, is an invitation to pause, to breathe, and to listen closely to the quiet and thunderous rhythms of human existence. The post Windows into life, love, and longing appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Abbas vows State police won’t be reduced to tools by governors as Reps rescind decision on bill
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The House of Representatives yesterday rescinded its earlier resolution on a constitutional amendment bill seeking to establish State Police Services. The post Abbas vows State police won’t be reduced to tools by governors as Reps rescind decision on bill appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: NANS Declares Emergency On Student Hostels, Moves Headquarters To Anambra
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The National Association of Nigerian Students (NANS) has declared a state of emergency over the poor condition of students' hostels across tertiary institutions in Nigeria, describing the situation as unacceptable. item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has announced a federal initiative to deliver more than 10,000 housing units across 14 states and the Federal Capital Territory, saying the programme will also generate in excess of 300,000 jobs as part of efforts to reduce Nigeria’s housing deficit. item.description: Trump's comments aired as the two countries exchanged fire for the fourth day in a row and the US resumed blockading Iranian ports.

item.description: As demand for charcoal threatens indigenous trees, communities are finding new value in what was once treated as waste.

item.description: The West Africa Telecommunications Regulators Assembly (WATRA) is moving to transform global recommendations on submarine cable resilience into a coordinated read more WATRA pushes regional action as global cable resilience plan takes shape

item.description: HOW many mass abductions have we recorded in Nigeria since Chibok in 2014? Can you count them? What kind of people are we? The post Mass abductions: Who gave birth to these demons? appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.description: Troops of Operation HADIN KAI have intensified offensive operations in Nigeria's North-East following the failed ISWAP attack on Cross Kauwa in Kukawa Local Government Area of Borno State, leading to the arrest of a suspected foreign terrorist operative. item.title: Passengers, operators lament as waterways transportation fares soar
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Waterways transport users on Tuesday lamented over the high cost of commuting on the Lagos inland waterways, stating that many now go by road for long-distance trips to avoid paying high waterways transportation cost. item.title: Solutions to mitigate the political abuse of state police, by Bashir Are
item.dc:creator: Guardian NG Opinion
item.description: The principal and most legitimate objection to state police is not constitutional but practical: the risk that governors could weaponise a state force against political opponents, journalists, or ethnic and religious minorities. This objection deserves a serious answer, not dismissal, The post Solut

item.title: Google and Epic give up fighting — third-party Android app stores are coming next week
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Epic Games and Google have just jointly withdrawn their attempt to retroactively settle the lawsuit that's changing how Android app stores work in the United States - and that means Google will be forced to carry rival app stores inside of its own. In fact, Google tells the court, it's ready to begi

item.title: Rescued Oyo pupils, teachers reunite with families
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: From Peter Moses (Lagos), Hussein Yahaya & Jide Olusola (Abuja)   Parents and relatives on Tuesday reunited with some of the 44 pupils and teachers recently rescued from kidnappers in Oyo State. Adeyemi was picked up on Tuesda

item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Oyo State Police Command has arrested three suspected members of a railway vandalism syndicate and recovered stolen rail tracks valued at about ₦200 million in a major crackdown on the vandalisation of critical national infrastructure. item.title: Beyond akara and corn: Reimagining wealth creation
item.dc:creator: Guardian NG Opinion
item.description: By Ojo Emmanuel Ademola Recent comments by Nigeria’s First Lady, Senator Oluremi Tinubu, encouraging unemployed women to consider small-scale enterprises such as akara and roasted corn sales have generated extensive public debate. While some critics have interpreted the advice as The post Beyond aka

item.title: Collateral consequences of President Tinubu’s healthcare reforms (2)
item.dc:creator: Guardian NG Opinion
item.description: By Okey Akpa Continued from yesterday The implications extend well beyond the factory floor. item.title: Bauchi APC executives defect to PRP
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Another wave of defection has struck Bauchi State chapter of the All Progressives Congress (APC) following the defection of Senator Shehu Buba and Samaila Kaila Dahuwa as the APC State Deputy Chairman, Ibrahim Makama Misau as the local government and ward executives in Misau Local Government Area de

item.description: Francis Sardauna in Katsina Former governor of Katsina State, Dr. Ibrahim Shehu Shema, has described the reforms taken by President Bola Tinubu as difficult but necessary in revamping the nation’s

item.title: Ekiti ADC will not challenge Oyebanji’s election
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The African Democratic Congress, ADC, candidate in the June 20 governorship election in Ekiti State, Amb Dare Bejide, and the party have resolved not to challenge the victory of the incumbent governor, Biodun Oyebanji, at the election petition tribunal in the state. Candidates have 21 days to file e

item.title: Ize-Iyamu concedes Edo South APC ticket
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: All Progressives Congress (APC) aspirant for Edo South Senatorial ticket, Pastor Osagie Ize-Iyamu, has conceded defeat in the recently conducted party primary. Pastor Ize-Iyamu and Hon. Omoregie Ogbeide-Ihama had both laid claim to the party’s senatorial ticket following separate primaries conducted
